title: Human Rights Campaign Fund established
description: Steve Endean set up the Human Rights Campaign Fund in 1980 to support candidates favorable to gay rights. It later grew into the largest LGBTQ civil rights organization in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Democratic Party adds gay rights plank to its platform
description: In 1980 the Democratic Party included support for homosexual rights in its national platform, the first major US political party to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rabbi Lionel Blue comes out as gay
description: Lionel Blue publicly acknowledged in 1980 that he was gay, a first for a rabbi in Britain.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First conference for Black lesbians held in San Francisco
description: "Becoming Visible: The First Black Lesbian Conference" took place at the Women's Building on October 17-19, 1980, and is credited as the earliest conference organized for African-American lesbians.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: David McReynolds runs as first openly gay US presidential candidate
description: The Socialist Party USA nominated David McReynolds for president in 1980, the first openly gay person to run for the office in American history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Victoria decriminalizes homosexual acts
description: The Australian state of Victoria removed criminal penalties for consensual sex between men in 1981, applying a uniform age of consent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Colombia decriminalizes same-sex relations
description: A new Colombian penal code in force from 1981 dropped the prohibition on consensual same-sex activity and set an equal age of consent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Norway enacts first national law against anti-gay discrimination
description: Norway amended its penal code in 1981 to prohibit discrimination against homosexuals, the first country in the world to adopt such protection.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: European court rules against Northern Ireland's ban on gay sex
description: In Dudgeon v United Kingdom, decided on October 22, 1981, the European Court of Human Rights held that Northern Ireland's criminalization of consensual sex between men breached the right to private life, opening the way to decriminalization the following year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Moral Majority begins campaign against homosexuality
description: The evangelical political organization Moral Majority launched an organized push against gay rights in the United States in 1981.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First gender-affirming surgery performed in Hong Kong
description: Hong Kong's first sex-reassignment operation was carried out in 1981, a medical first for the territory.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US CDC publishes first report of the illness later named AIDS
description: On June 5, 1981, the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention described a cluster of rare pneumonia cases among gay men, the earliest official documentation of the condition later called AIDS.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Billie Jean King outed as first prominent lesbian professional athlete
description: A palimony lawsuit filed by Marilyn Barnett in May 1981 made public her relationship with tennis champion Billie Jean King, who thereby became the first prominent professional athlete known to be lesbian. King lost all of her endorsement deals as a result.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mary C. Morgan appointed as first openly gay US judge
description: California Governor Jerry Brown named Mary C. Morgan to the San Francisco Municipal Court in 1981, making her the first openly gay or lesbian judge in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Randy Shilts becomes first openly gay reporter in US mainstream press
description: The San Francisco Chronicle hired Randy Shilts as a national correspondent in 1981, the first openly gay journalist covering gay issues at a mainstream American newspaper.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: NYPD sergeant Charles Cochrane comes out at council hearing
description: Sergeant Charles H. Cochrane Jr. declared he was gay during a New York City Council hearing in 1981, the first member of the city's police department to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: London Bisexual Group starts as first UK bisexual organization
description: The London Bisexual Group formed in 1981, the first organization for bisexual people in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: LGBTQ health-professional association GLMA formed
description: The organization now known as GLMA: Health Professionals Advancing LGBTQ+ Equality began in 1981 and grew into the world's oldest and largest membership body of LGBTQ+ and allied health professionals.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: France equalizes the age of consent
description: In 1982 France repealed the higher age of consent that had applied to homosexual acts, placing same-sex and different-sex relations on the same legal footing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Northern Ireland decriminalizes sex between men
description: Following the European Court of Human Rights ruling in Dudgeon v United Kingdom, Northern Ireland removed criminal penalties for consensual homosexual acts in 1982.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Portugal decriminalizes homosexual acts for the second time
description: Portugal removed consensual same-sex activity from its criminal law in 1982, restoring a decriminalization it had first enacted in the nineteenth century.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Wisconsin becomes first US state to outlaw anti-gay discrimination
description: In 1982 Wisconsin passed the first law of any US state banning discrimination against homosexuals.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New South Wales enacts Australia's first anti-discrimination law for gay people
description: New South Wales outlawed discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived homosexuality in 1982, the first Australian state to adopt such protection.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ken Togo launches the Deracine Party in Japan
description: Activist Ken Togo started the Deracine Party in Japan in 1982, an early political vehicle for gay visibility in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gay Officers Action League formed in New York
description: NYPD Sergeant Charles H. Cochrane Jr. and former New Jersey sergeant Sam Ciccone created the Gay Officers Action League (GOAL) in 1982, the first group dedicated to gay members of law enforcement.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laguna Beach elects the first openly gay mayor in the United States
description: The city of Laguna Beach, California chose an openly gay mayor in 1982, a first in American history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Dickerson wins Mr. Olympia as an openly gay competitor
description: Bodybuilder Chris Dickerson took the Mr. Olympia title in 1982, the first openly gay athlete to hold it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Gay Games take place in San Francisco
description: The inaugural Gay Games drew about 1,600 athletes to San Francisco in 1982, starting what became a recurring international sporting event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: CDC adopts the term AIDS
description: In September 1982 the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention began using the name AIDS for the syndrome previously labeled GRID or "gay cancer", reporting that one to two new American cases were diagnosed each day.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Guernsey decriminalizes homosexual acts
description: The Bailiwick of Guernsey, including Alderney, Herm and Sark, ended criminal penalties for consensual sex between men in 1983.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bisexual political organization BiPOL starts in San Francisco
description: Autumn Courtney, Lani Ka'ahumanu, Arlene Krantz, David Lourea, Bill Mack, Alan Rockway and Maggi Rubenstein created BiPOL in San Francisco in 1983, America's first bisexual political organization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gerry Studds becomes first openly gay member of the US Congress
description: Massachusetts Representative Gerry Studds acknowledged his homosexuality on the floor of the House in 1983, the first sitting member of Congress to come out.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jerry Falwell brands AIDS a "gay plague"
description: Televangelist Jerry Falwell publicly described AIDS as a "gay plague" in 1983, deepening stigma against gay men in the early years of the epidemic.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Herb Donaldson named California's first openly gay male judge
description: Governor Jerry Brown appointed Herb Donaldson to a municipal court in 1983, making him the first openly gay man to serve as a judge in California.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: David Scondras elected to Boston City Council as openly gay man
description: David Scondras won a seat on the Boston City Council in 1983, the first openly gay official elected to that body.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kitty Tsui publishes first book by an out Chinese American lesbian
description: With Words of a Woman Who Breathes Fire (1983), Kitty Tsui became the first known Chinese American lesbian to publish a book.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Anita Cornwell publishes Black Lesbian in White America
description: Anita Cornwell's Black Lesbian in White America appeared in 1983, the first published essay collection by an African-American lesbian.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New South Wales and the Northern Territory legalize homosexual acts
description: In 1984 both New South Wales and the Northern Territory removed criminal penalties for consensual sex between men, extending decriminalization across more of Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ten Percent Club forms in Hong Kong
description: The Ten Percent Club began in Hong Kong in 1984, an early organization for the territory's gay community.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Argentine Homosexual Community forms as national umbrella group
description: Comunidad Homosexual Argentina (CHA) brought several pre-existing groups together in 1984, creating Argentina's leading gay advocacy organization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ILGA Japan established
description: A Japanese branch of the International Lesbian and Gay Association started work in 1984, linking local activists to the international movement.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Massachusetts voters re-elect Gerry Studds after his coming-out
description: Representative Gerry Studds won re-election in 1984 with the electorate fully aware of his homosexuality, having come out the previous year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Smith becomes first openly gay man in the UK Parliament
description: Newly elected Labour MP Chris Smith declared in 1984 that he was gay, the first openly gay male politician in the UK Parliament; Maureen Colquhoun had earlier been the first openly out politician in the country in 1975.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Berkeley pioneers domestic-partner benefits for city workers
description: In 1984 Berkeley, California became the first US city to offer domestic partnership health benefits to municipal employees.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: West Hollywood incorporates with a majority-gay city council
description: The newly created city of West Hollywood, California elected a city council in 1984 with an openly gay or lesbian majority, the first city known to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Reconstructionist Judaism opens clergy roles to gay and lesbian Jews
description: In 1984 Reconstructionist Judaism became the first Jewish denomination to accept openly gay and lesbian rabbis and cantors.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lesbian erotica magazine On Our Backs debuts
description: Debi Sundahl and Myrna Elana launched On Our Backs in 1984, the first women-run erotica magazine in the United States and the first aimed at a lesbian readership, with contributors including Susie Bright, Joan Nestle and Patrick Califia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First bisexual rights rally held outside Democratic convention
description: BiPOL organized the first rally for bisexual rights outside the 1984 Democratic National Convention in San Francisco, with nine speakers from allied civil rights groups.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: France bans lifestyle-based discrimination in work and services
description: A 1985 French law prohibited discrimination on the grounds of way of life in employment and in access to goods and services, extending legal protection to gay people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Belgium equalizes the age of consent
description: Belgium set the same age of consent for same-sex and different-sex activity in 1985, removing the higher threshold that had applied to homosexual acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Restoration Church of Jesus Christ and Bisexual Resource Center start
description: Two US organizations began in 1985: the Restoration Church of Jesus Christ, an LGBTQ-affirming denomination in the Latter-day Saint tradition, and the Bisexual Resource Center in Massachusetts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First memorial to gay victims of the Holocaust dedicated
description: A memorial commemorating homosexual men persecuted and killed under the Nazi regime was dedicated in 1985, the first monument of its kind.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rock Hudson dies of AIDS-related illness
description: Film star Rock Hudson died in 1985, the first major public figure known to have died of an AIDS-related illness; his death sharply raised public attention to the epidemic.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Terry Sweeney joins Saturday Night Live as openly gay cast member
description: Terry Sweeney, who was already out, became Saturday Night Live's first openly gay male cast member in 1985.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: British soap Brookside introduces openly gay character
description: The Liverpool-set serial Brookside featured the first openly gay character on a British television series in 1985.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Zealand legalizes sex between men
description: The Homosexual Law Reform Act 1986 ended criminal penalties in New Zealand for consensual sex between males aged 16 and over.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
